name: Strait of Hormuz
summary: Day 77 of Hormuz blockade. **TRUMP-XI JOINT STATEMENT NAMES HORMUZ DIRECTLY: 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED — ToI May 15 liveblog primary, China MFA readout). Xi additionally opposed 'the militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use (a direct rhetorical strike at Mokhber's 'atomic-bomb' doctrine), and said China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait. **THE STRAIT REMAINS PHYSICALLY CLOSED** despite the joint statement: ~30 vessels/day vs ~140 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser line unchanged), Iran is proactively curbing output because storage tanks are filling, and EIA confirmed Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026. **Diplomatic words don't reopen shipping lanes that IRGC mines and small-boat attacks are physically closing.** Trump-Xi joint statement is the third repetition of 'Hormuz must remain open' since February — strait has remained largely blocked. **TWO NEW KINETIC INCIDENTS ON SUMMIT DAY**: **B1 Honduras-flagged HUI CHUAN seized off Fujairah by 'unauthorised personnel,' reportedly bound for Iranian territorial waters** (CONFIRMED on seizure event — UKMTO primary advisory; REPORTED on Iranian destination — UKMTO investigating, no claim of responsibility). Vessel identified by BBC; Fox News reports possible 'floating armory.' Cycle's second confirmed Iranian-direction seizure on a vessel previously at anchor in UAE waters. Iran has not claimed responsibility — consistent with prior pattern of operational ambiguity. The simultaneity with the joint statement IS the signal. **B2 Indian-flagged dhow MSV HAJ ALI sunk off Oman in suspected drone attack** (CONFIRMED sinking — Bloomberg, Business Standard, MEA primary; CLAIMED on drone attribution — no confirmed attribution). 14 crew rescued by Omani Coast Guard. India MEA called the attack 'unacceptable' — strongest public Indian language on a Gulf incident in the cycle. **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED via Baltic Exchange Week 17 report at WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** — Lloyd's List $420-462K range earlier this week is now corroborated; Week 4 (late January) was WS126.5/TCE $112,394/day, so today's rate is ~4.2x late-January and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. Frontline (FRO) tape selling on de-escalation conviction while spot rate is at multi-year highs — paper-physical divergence at cycle extreme. **A11 HORMUZ/RED SEA NEGATIVE SPACE HOLDS**: No fresh UKMTO advisory; Project Freedom Day 10 paused; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; Qatari-EEZ Safesea Neha strike now Day 5 of 7 with no claim of responsibility and no Qatar MoFA statement. **EIA MAY STEO assumption: Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June; Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Brent $89 4Q26.** AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day = ~8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% baseline. Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' doctrine UNCHANGED but now diplomatically isolated — Xi rhetorically attacked the Hormuz toll-booth premise. **A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE (carrying forward): Day 4 of 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed (no 2nd Red Sea incident); Day 5 of 7 since May 10 Safesea Neha Qatari EEZ strike (no 2nd strike); Project Freedom pause Day 10; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; no 5th disabled Iranian-flagged tanker. UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page does not log a second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 13-14 morning.** **A9 BLOOMBERG MAY 13 SATELLITE: KHARG ISLAND OIL JETTIES EMPTY AGAIN** = second satellite snapshot of empty jetties this cycle, operational confirmation of Iranian export collapse without kinetic premium. The National (Abu Dhabi) ran sober analytical piece May 13 explicitly NOT attributing slick to Iran ('where did it come from?') — Gulf-press editorial caution maintained. **EIA SEPARATELY CONFIRMED (Bloomberg May 13): Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026** = official-data confirmation of Lloyd's List operational tracker series. **B3 EIA MAY STEO (CONFIRMED PRIMARY): assumption Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June; Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Brent $89 4Q26.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line carries forward: 2-5 ships/day Hormuz (vs ~6/day May 10 / ~70 pre-war), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June. **VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K) = MAJOR escalation signal — paper-Brent at $105.68 vs spot VLCC at $420K is the cleanest paper-physical divergence indicator we have. AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day = 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%; LMA confirms market available (88% appetite hull, 90% cargo); some Hormuz crossings paying up to 10% in mid-March extreme cases. **FBX13 (China/East Asia → Mediterranean) at $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) carries forward; container freight tanker-container divergence persists.** Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz doctrine UNCHANGED. PRIOR (May 13): **A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE INTELLIGENCE — NO 2ND RED SEA INCIDENT in 7-day window from May 11 Hodeidah (Day 2 of 7); single unclaimed remains; NO 2ND STRIKE inside Qatari EEZ following May 10 Safesea Neha (Day 3 of 7); Project Freedom pause holds Day 8 (since May 5-6); CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; NO 5TH DISABLED TANKER. UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page does not log a second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 12-13 morning. The non-event IS the intelligence: 75 days into Hormuz blockade and 8 days into Project Freedom pause, the kinetic ceiling held through diplomatic-rejection shock — Iran did NOT use the opening to escalate maritime pressure on either Qatari coastline or Bab el-Mandeb, which would have been the predictable retaliation playbook.** **A9 KHARG SLICK CONSOLIDATED VIA COPERNICUS: Al Jazeera + ynet + Jerusalem Post + Conflict and Environment Observatory now amplify Sentinel-1/-2/-3 imagery showing ~45 sq km west of Kharg, May 6-8 detection — the largest since the war began 70 days ago. Iranian VP Shina Ansari publicly denied Iranian-facility origin: 'foreign tanker dumping ballast water.' Fox May 11-12 ~12-20 sq km second slick is consistent with drift south rather than separate origin event — re-appearance trigger AMBIGUOUSLY ACTIVATED (drift ≠ re-appearance, ballast counter-claim not formally falsified). Western wires in slow-amplification mode on attribution.** **B3 ARAMCO STRATEGIC NARRATIVE CONFIRMED — Aramco Q1 +25% YoY off East-West pipeline reroute (Yanbu exports 770K bpd Jan-Feb → ~4 MMbpd late March → 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May; East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd capacity); Aramco + ADNOC reportedly 'quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports' (World Oil May 8). Saudi-Emirati supply ~90% preserved through non-Hormuz routing + quiet limited Hormuz transits, even as headline non-Iranian Hormuz throughput shows -96% (EIA: avg ~500,000 bpd non-Iranian crude since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war). Explains why Brent has not gone parabolic — Gulf-state pipeline optionality is the saving grace.** **A13 KOREA HMM NAMU MAY 4 INCIDENT: South Korean government formally CONFIRMED EXTERNAL STRIKE — investigation found TWO unidentified airborne objects struck stern at ~1 min intervals ('double-tap' drone pattern); mine + torpedo formally ruled out. FM Cho Hyun May 13: 'too early to determine who was responsible.' Opposition PPP publicly criticised government for omitting Iran. Seoul institutionally maintaining 'various actors' hedge.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line ('2-5 ships/day, market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz disruption persists past mid-June') carries forward. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline); trigger 'AWRP normalisation toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 7 of 10 — FAILING HARDER. VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day; Frontline +1.0% 1D / +10.6% 1M / +28.6% 3M (insurers + tanker market have NOT bought de-escalation). FBX13 China/E.Asia → Med container rate $4,179 week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity caught up; tanker-container divergence persists. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **HORMUZ THROUGHPUT DETERIORATED TO 2-5 SHIPS/DAY PER ARAMCO CEO NASSER MAY 11 (vs ~6/day May 10 / ~70 pre-war / ~138 baseline normal); 100M bbl/week lost; 880M cumulative; market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** (CNBC + Yahoo + Eastern Herald — industry-CEO authority on these numbers is significant; Nasser is unlikely to overstate disruption which hurts his customer base). **A11 CENTCOM PRECISION TALLY 58→61 REDIRECTED + 4 IRANIAN-FLAGGED TANKERS DISABLED COUNT HOLDS** (3 redirects in 48h = below early-May pace, Project Freedom remains paused since May 5/6). CENTCOM separately citing 'more than 70 tankers' prevented + '$13 billion in oil that will never reach market.' **B17 VLCC AGIOS FANOURIOS I (Iraqi Basra crude, bound Vietnam Nghi Son) REVERSED COURSE BACK INTO GULF OF OMAN AT ~5 KNOTS WITHOUT CROSSING US BLOCKADE LINE** = visible confidence-collapse signature even for compliant non-Iranian VLCCs (Bloomberg + gCaptain May 11). **A4 SECOND KHARG SLICK DETECTED MAY 11-12** (~12-20 sq km, smaller than May 6-8 45-65 sq km but PERSISTENCE elevates storage-overflow hypothesis vs European-tanker-waste counter-claim; UN warned 'major disaster' risk; trigger 'Kharg slick re-appears within 14 days' Day 1 of 14 has FIRED on persistence). **A3 SECOND COMMERCIAL VESSEL STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER (May 10 + 7 days = NOT TRIPPED through May 12 Day 2 of 7);** VESSEL ATTRIBUTION OF MAY 10 STRIKE CLARIFIED: Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed by NJ-based Safesea Group (provides logistics for UN peacekeeping, WFP, GSA — strike on US-managed vessel during peace talks materially more politically loaded than May 10 unflagged framing); this is 2nd Safesea vessel hit this cycle (Safesea Vishnu, March 12 STS Iraqi waters, killed one Indian crew); NO party has claimed; Pravda attribution to two Iranian UAVs UNCONFIRMED by Western forensics. **A9 HOUTHI DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11** — UKMTO confirmed multiple explosions near a vessel 130 km (80 mi) SW of Hodeidah, Yemen; no injuries; ship continued voyage; vessel briefly 'not under command'; HOUTHIS DID NOT CLAIM RESPONSIBILITY; non-claim itself a signal. Critically breaks cycle's most stable non-event: 80+ days of zero Red Sea attacks during multi-axis kinetic restart had been one of strongest structural ceilings. If second Red Sea incident occurs within next 7 days, Houthi non-action read collapses. **B6 WSJ MAY 11: UAE AIR FORCE SECRETLY STRUCK IRAN'S LAVAN ISLAND OIL REFINERY APRIL 8 USING MIRAGE 2000-9** (55,000 bpd offline months) — reframes prior Iranian UAE-targeted strikes as direct retaliation against confirmed third combatant; net Iranian oil-infrastructure damage retroactively confirmed (Lavan capacity offline). **TRUMP CHINA VISIT MAY 14-15 + WH Sit-Room May 11 weighing renewed Project Freedom restart** = pause window over 1-week clock close. Trinary regime is now stabilizing into sustainable operational pattern: US blockade fully in force (footprint holding/reinforcing despite Ford rotation); Iran selectively enforcing PGSA rules at Hormuz; both sides engaging kinetically; persistent commercial-vessel attacks now in Qatari EEZ (perpetrator UNKNOWN) + possible Red Sea reactivation. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline); trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 — failing hard. Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' doctrine UNCHANGED. **HORMUZ REOPENING TRIGGER ('transit volume above 30/day for 3 consecutive days') failing harder — 2-5/day per Aramco.** **B1 BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING — FIRST COMMERCIAL VESSEL ATTACK SINCE CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO (MAY 5) AND FIRST NEAR QATAR'S COAST.** Well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan; small fire extinguished, no casualties; UKMTO Warning notice posted; vessel flag/owner not publicly disclosed; NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY (consistent with May 5 'claim-management discrimination pattern'). Operationally consistent with IRGC signal to Qatar that mediator role does not buy immunity for blockade-aligned shipping. **B2 KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK (NEW — first hard-data signal blockade biting at production-export interface):** Sentinel-1/2/3 imagery May 6-8 shows large slick west of Kharg (~45km surface extent); Iran (lawmaker via Iran International/Maariv) blames European tanker waste; Western analysts (Fox-quoted) suggest blockade pressure forcing storage-overflow dumping. CAUSE CONTESTED but the slick is operationally confirmed (CONFIRMED satellite imagery — Copernicus, Anadolu, JPost, NewsNation, France 24). **MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' DOCTRINE UNCHANGED — no new statement; institutional intelligence durable.** This is the cleanest possible operational answer to yesterday's question of which track wins (operational reality vs MOU 'both sides lift blockades' rhetoric) — direction is overwhelmingly toward the PGSA regime, not the MOU regime (CONFIRMED — Lloyd's List Intelligence via CNN, Gulf News, HormuzStraitMonitor.com). The 1,500-vessel figure is the largest reported standstill of the cycle. **MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' DOCTRINE INSTITUTIONALIZES SELECTIVE HORMUZ CONTROL AS PERMANENT STRATEGIC ASSET (NEW — MAJOR INSTITUTIONAL):** Mohammad Mokhber (adviser to supreme leader, described as Mojtaba's adviser): 'The Strait of Hormuz represents an opportunity as precious as an atomic bomb. Indeed, having in one's hands a position that allows you to influence the global economy with a single decision is a major opportunity.' Pledged Iran 'will change the [legal] regime of this strait — through international law if possible, and unilaterally if not' (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel, L'Orient Today, Middle East Eye, Deccan Herald). First time a leadership-adjacent figure has publicly endorsed unilateral codification of selective Hormuz control. Reads as institutional intelligence: even if MOU is signed, Iran intends to retain operational regime of Hormuz transit control as a permanent strategic asset — directly contradicts MOU's 'BOTH sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision. **CENTCOM SECOND DAY OF TANKER STRIKES + BLOCKADE SCALE FORMALIZED:** US Navy F/A-18 from USS George H.W. Bush disabled M/T Sea Star III and M/T Sevda May 8 (precision-munition into smokestacks, same tactic as M/T Hasna May 7); CENTCOM disclosed 'more than 70 tankers' held under blockade, 20 warships, 200 aircraft, 15,000 troops enforcing — 'fully in effect' (CONFIRMED — UPI, Stars and Stripes, NPR, CBS, Fox News, TWZ). Iran claimed counter-seizure of unidentified tanker in Gulf of Oman. The trinary regime is the operating reality: US blockade fully in force, Iran selectively enforcing PGSA rules at Hormuz, both sides engaging kinetically against each other's vessels, both sides claiming the ceasefire holds. Yesterday's 'love tap' framing did NOT survive a single 24-hour cycle. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day, 6-8x pre-war norm — insurers have NOT bought the May 5 pause as durable; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 3/10 — failing. Brent close $101.73 May 8 (+1.66% 1D); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden; UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED. Hormuz reopening trigger ('transit volume above 30/day for 3 consecutive days') failing harder — zero transits May 7.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz reopens to all traffic (even partially)
US attempts naval escort or forced opening of Hormuz

War risk insurance for Gulf transits breaks below 0.5% hull value (normalization threshold) — REFRAMED 2026-05-06: 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions' is now the diplomatic-track-durability test under the Trump pause framework
Iran formally reopens Hormuz to all traffic within 14-21 days of May 5 pause (by 2026-05-26) — would collapse tanker spot rates -15-25%
Second commercial vessel attack within 7 days inside Qatari EEZ following Sunday May 10 bulk carrier strike — confirms IRGC graduated-pressure-on-mediator-coastline pattern
Kharg Island slick re-appears on subsequent satellite passes (Copernicus Sentinel) within 14 days — confirms Iran storage-overflow / regime-dumping hypothesis vs European-tanker-waste counter-claim
active_triggers.activation_note: Second slick detected May 11-12 (~12-20 sq km, smaller but persistent; UN warned 'major disaster' risk). Re-appearance has now occurred. Hypothesis tilt toward storage-overflow / regime-dumping has strengthened modestly; CAUSE remains formally contested (Iran maintains European tanker waste claim).

active_triggers.condition: Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory — would collapse Houthi non-action read
active_triggers.note: Day 1 of 7. May 11 Hodeidah incident was UNCLAIMED. If second incident occurs, the cycle's most stable non-event (80+ days of zero Red Sea attacks) collapses.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz remains under Iranian selective control even within ceasefire framework — ACTIVE 2026-04-08: Iran's 'controlled passage via coordination with armed forces' framing in the ceasefire text formali
Physical-futures gap closes below $10/bbl (Dated Brent converges to Brent futures)

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP-XI JOINT STATEMENT NAMES HORMUZ DIRECTLY: 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED — ToI May 15 liveblog primary, China MFA readout). Xi opposed 'the militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use (direct rhetorical strike at Mokhber's 'atomic-bomb' doctrine); said China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait. **THE STRAIT REMAINS PHYSICALLY CLOSED**: ~30 vessels/day vs ~140 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser unchanged), Iran is proactively curbing output because storage tanks are filling, EIA confirmed Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026. **Diplomatic words don't reopen shipping lanes that IRGC mines and small-boat attacks are physically closing.** **B1 HUI CHUAN SEIZURE — UKMTO advisory May 14: Honduras-flagged vessel taken by 'unauthorised personnel whilst at anchor' 38nm NE of Fujairah, 'now bound for Iranian territorial waters'** (CONFIRMED on event — UKMTO primary; REPORTED on Iranian destination — investigating; NO claim of responsibility). BBC identified the vessel; Fox News reports possible 'floating armory.' Cycle's second confirmed Iranian-direction seizure on a vessel previously at anchor in UAE waters. **B2 MSV HAJ ALI sunk off Oman in suspected drone attack** (CONFIRMED sinking — Bloomberg, Business Standard, MEA primary; CLAIMED attribution). All 14 crew rescued by Omani Coast Guard. India MEA called the attack 'unacceptable' = strongest public Indian language on a Gulf incident in the cycle. **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED via Baltic Exchange Week 17 report at WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** — Lloyd's List $420-462K range earlier this week corroborated; ~4.2x late-January reading and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. Markets dossier flags Week 18 partial: WS408.13 (Lloyd's 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade') — still ~9x pre-war. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger added yesterday at watching: basis CONFIRMED ($467K far above $200K), 5-session sustain pending daily-print verification — upgrade to confirmed_basis_pending_sustain.** **A11 HORMUZ/RED SEA NEGATIVE SPACE HOLDS**: No fresh UKMTO advisory; Project Freedom Day 10 paused; CENTCOM redirect tally 61; Safesea Neha strike Day 5 of 7, no claim, no Qatar MoFA statement. **B3 MODI-MBZ ENERGY MoUs (LPG + Strategic Petroleum Reserves) + FUJAIRAH EXPANSION ALTERNATIVE-ROUTE PLAN PUBLICLY DISCLOSED** = India operational alternative-route engineering as Hormuz-bypass strategy. UAE Minister Sultan Al Jaber: 'energy has been weaponised by Hormuz being taken hostage effectively' = cleanest Gulf-state public language framing Hormuz situation as weaponisation by Iran. **EIA May STEO: Hormuz assumed effectively closed until late May; Gulf states shut in 10.5 MMbpd April; Brent $89 4Q26.** AWRP ~1% H&M (8x pre-war). Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' doctrine unchanged but now diplomatically isolated — Xi rhetorically attacked the toll-booth premise.
sources: Times of Israel May 15 liveblog (joint statement primary, Hormuz language)
Baltic Exchange Week 17 Tanker Report (VLCC TD3C primary)
The National (Abu Dhabi) — UAE Minister Sultan Al Jaber 'weaponised' language

recent_signals.verification: NO 2nd Red Sea incident Day 2 of 7 + NO 2nd Qatari EEZ Day 3 of 7 = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO + Lloyd's List); Project Freedom pause Day 8 + CENTCOM 61 unchanged = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; Kharg slick ~45 sq km May 6-8 = CONFIRMED Copernicus (multi-amplified); second slick consistency with drift = PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (Fox primary, mainstream lagging); re-appearance trigger AMBIGUOUSLY activated; HMM Namu external strike + double-tap drone pattern = CONFIRMED (Seoul official briefing); attribution = UNCONFIRMED (FM Cho hedge); Aramco Q1 +25% + ADNOC quiet Hormuz resume = CONFIRMED (corporate financials + World Oil industry); EIA non-Iranian Hormuz ~500K bpd avg = CONFIRMED (EIA primary).

recent_signals.headline: HORMUZ THROUGHPUT DETERIORATED TO 2-5 SHIPS/DAY PER ARAMCO CEO NASSER MAY 11 (vs ~6/day May 10, ~70 pre-war); 100M bbl/week lost; 880M cumulative; market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June. CENTCOM tally 58→61 redirected + 4 disabled holds (3 redirects in 48h below early-May pace — Project Freedom remains paused since May 5/6); footprint USS Bush + USS Lincoln + 20+ warships + 200+ aircraft + 15,000 troops; USS Gerald R. Ford transiting Gibraltar westbound (Day 322, home to Norfolk); USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU imminent arrival; George Washington CSG departed Yokosuka May 10 — footprint holding/reinforcing despite Ford rotation. **VLCC Agios Fanourios I (Iraqi Basra crude bound Vietnam Nghi Son) reversed course back into Gulf of Oman at ~5 knots without crossing US blockade line** = confidence-collapse signature even for compliant non-Iranian VLCCs (Bloomberg + gCaptain). SECOND KHARG SLICK DETECTED MAY 11-12 (~12-20 sq km, persistent; UN warned 'major disaster' risk) — Kharg re-appearance trigger ACTIVATED, hypothesis tilt toward storage-overflow/regime-dumping strengthened. Second commercial vessel attack inside Qatari EEZ trigger NOT TRIPPED Day 2 of 7. VESSEL ATTRIBUTION OF MAY 10 STRIKE CLARIFIED: Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed Safesea Group (NJ — UN peacekeeping/WFP/GSA logistics); 2nd Safesea vessel hit cycle (Safesea Vishnu March 12 Iraqi waters killed one Indian crew); NO claim; Pravda attribution to two Iranian UAVs UNCONFIRMED by Western forensics. **HOUTHI DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11** — UKMTO confirmed multiple explosions near vessel 130km SW of Hodeidah; no injuries; vessel briefly 'not under command'; NO Houthi claim (the non-claim itself a signal). UAE LAVAN APRIL 8 STRIKE CONFIRMED VIA WSJ MAY 11 — 55,000 bpd capacity offline months (UAE Mirage 2000-9 aircraft). New OFAC sanctions wave May 11-12: 12 entities (4 HK, 4 UAE, 1 Oman) + 3 individuals on IRGC Shaid Purja'fari + Golden Globe Turkey-cover company. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%) — insurers have NOT bought peace story; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 FAILING HARD. Brent $105.58 May 12 (+1.3% 1D). Container freight separately: FBX13 China/East Asia → Mediterranean = $4,179 week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity has caught up; tanker market diverging from container market.

recent_signals.headline: BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — FIRST COMMERCIAL VESSEL ATTACK SINCE CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO MAY 5; FIRST NEAR QATAR'S COAST; SMALL FIRE EXTINGUISHED, NO CASUALTIES; UKMTO WARNING NOTICE; NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY); KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK MAY 6-8 (NEW — FIRST HARD-DATA SIGNAL BLOCKADE BITING AT PRODUCTION-EXPORT INTERFACE; CAUSE CONTESTED — Iran blames European tanker waste, Fox-quoted experts suggest storage-overflow dumping); CENTCOM PRECISION TALLY 58 VESSELS REDIRECTED + 4 IRANIAN-FLAGGED TANKERS DISABLED SINCE APRIL 13 (UPDATED — refines yesterday's >70-tankers-held framing; 4 disabled tankers: M/T Hasna May 7, M/T Sea Star III + M/T Sevda May 8, 4th identity not publicly disclosed); LLOYD'S LIST TRANSIT ~6 VESSELS/DAY VS ~60 BASELINE (latest data point); ~1,500 COMMERCIAL VESSELS ANCHORED. Bulk carrier strike geographic significance is acute: 23nm NE of Doha is well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan, ordinarily considered the safest waters in the Persian Gulf. Three reading frames operationally consistent: (i) IRGC pressure-tactic on mediator coastline concurrent with al-Thani Miami meeting; (ii) Houthi-style proxy operation (LOW probability — wrong geometry); (iii) misfire/accidental engagement. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day baseline (6-8x pre-war norm) — insurers have NOT bought peace story; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 4 of 10 — failing harder. Brent close $101.29 May 8 (yfinance); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. Lloyd's reports ~10% of normal Hormuz throughput. UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED. The trinary regime is now stabilizing into sustainable operational pattern: US blockade fully in force, Iran selectively enforcing PGSA rules, both sides engaging kinetically against each other's vessels and now near mediator coastline.
sources: UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
Iran International (Iran lawmaker denial via Maariv)

recent_signals.headline: ZERO HORMUZ TRANSITS MAY 7 + ~1,500 VESSELS ANCHORED IN PERSIAN GULF (largest standstill of cycle); CENTCOM DISCLOSED >70 TANKERS HELD UNDER BLOCKADE + 20 WARSHIPS, 200 AIRCRAFT, 15,000 TROOPS ENFORCING; SECOND DAY OF US TANKER STRIKES (M/T SEA STAR III + M/T SEVDA, MAY 8); MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' DOCTRINE INSTITUTIONALIZES SELECTIVE HORMUZ CONTROL AS PERMANENT STRATEGIC ASSET. The 7→0 transit count is a meaningful operational deterioration. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M (6-8x pre-war), 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' trigger Day 3/10 — failing. Mokhber (described as Mojtaba's adviser): 'Strait of Hormuz represents an opportunity as precious as an atomic bomb'; pledged Iran 'will change the [legal] regime of this strait — through international law if possible, and unilaterally if not' — first leadership-adjacent voice publicly endorsing unilateral codification of selective Hormuz control as permanent strategic asset; substantively contradicts MOU's 'BOTH sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision. CENTCOM blockade scale formally disclosed for first time: '20 warships stocked with over 200 aircraft and 15,000 troops are carrying out the blockade, which remains fully in effect.' Brent close $101.73 May 8 (+1.66% 1D); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden — UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED at 51-nation Paris summit (April 17) framework.

recent_signals.headline: MOU FRAMEWORK INCLUDES EXPLICIT 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES'; IRAN SIGNALED FIRST UNCONDITIONAL REOPENING OFFER OF CYCLE — PBS/AP/AL JAZEERA cite Iranian officials offering to 'reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade and war ends'; IRGC Navy added 'safe transit ensured with new procedures.' historical_summaries.summary: 12 signals. IRGC announced Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships | Dallas Federal Reserve described closure as 'largest disruption to energy supply since the 1970s energy crisis' and 'largest in the history | Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement confirmed Hormuz would remain closed to pressure Iran's enemies

64 signals. WSJ paradigm shift CORROBORATED by CNN: 'Trump administration believes it will have to end Iran war with oil chokepoint still closed | Al-Salmi VLCC attacked at Dubai port anchorage — 31 nautical miles NW of Dubai, OUTSIDE Hormuz proper | CONFIRMED: Hormuz toll law passed FULL Majlis (not just committee)
